servinfo.title: Fuel Tank Removal and Installation
servinfo.ptxt: Before performing the following procedure, observe Precautions for Fuel System Service .

s1.ptxt: Relieve fuel pressure from fuel feed line.
Disconnect negative (–) cable at battery.
Remove fuel filler cap.
Insert hose of a hand operated pump into fuel filler hose (1) and pump out fuel in space “A” shown in figure.

attention1.ptxt: There is a risk of fire or explosion if the drained fuel is stored in an inappropriate container.
Avoid using an open container to hold the fuel.

attention4.ptxt: The hose of the hand-operated pump could damage the fuel tank inlet valve if too much force is used when inserting it into the fuel filler hose.
Do not apply too strong a force when inserting the pump hose into the fuel filler hose.

s1.ptxt: Hoist vehicle, and remove fuel filler neck lower cover (1).
Remove clamps (2), fuel filler hose (1) and breather hose (3) from fuel filler neck.
Remove exhaust center pipe.
Disconnect quick joints (1) from fuel pipes (2).
Support fuel tank (1) with jack (2) and remove its mounting bolts.
Lower fuel tank a little to disconnect fuel pump connector (1), then remove fuel tank.

attention4.ptxt: Applying tension to the fuel pump harness will cause damage to the fuel pump and the fuel pump harness when lowering the fuel tank.
Disconnect the fuel pump connector with the fuel tank lowered to a height at which tension is not applied to the fuel pump harness.

attention1.ptxt: Failure to take proper precautions when reinstalling the fuel tank connections can result in fuel leakage or damage.
Clean the outside surface of the pipe where each quick joint is to be installed, push the pipe into the joint until the joint lock clicks, and then confirm that the pipe is connected securely.

s1.ptxt: Raise fuel tank (1) with jack (2) and connect fuel pump connector and clamp wire harness.
Install fuel tank to vehicle and tighten new fuel tank bolts to specified torque.

attention4.ptxt: The fuel tank bolts are pre-coated with friction stabilizer. If the bolts are reused, they may work loose.
Be sure to use the new fuel tank bolts.

s1.ptxt: Connect fuel filler hose (1) and breather hose (2) to filler neck (3) as shown in figure, and clamp them securely.
For proper installation, refer to Fuel Hose Disconnection and Reconnection .
Install fuel filler neck lower cover (1).
Connect fuel feed hose (1) and vapor hose (2) to each pipe as shown in figure, and clamp them securely.
Install fuel filler cap.
Install exhaust center pipe.
Connect negative (–) cable at battery.
With engine stopped, set ignition ON and check for fuel leaks.
